# ADR-004 Compliance — Data Architecture

**Governance phase:** 3.5 · **Authority document:** `docs/ADR-004-*.md` · **Date:** 2026-07-19
**Verifier:** `architecture/compliance/verify-architecture.mjs` · **Do not edit the ADR itself.**

## Purpose
Name the nature of information: facts (immutable) vs state (replaceable) vs derivations (disposable); one authoritative owner per fact; corrections as new facts.

## Scope
Enterprise data architecture — data classes, source-of-truth, immutability, retention. No DB/schema/SQL prescription.

## Current implementation status
**Proposed; Instincts present, formalization pending — 30% compliant.**

## Implemented components
- Append-only transaction ledger (facts)
- Append-only driver approval audit log
- Effective JWT revocation records
- Read models isolated behind Infrastructure adapters (derivations)

## Remaining work
- Formal data-class tagging platform-wide
- Postgres cutover (single-writer SQLite is the ceiling)
- Governed-deletion / retention / legal-hold lifecycle
- Snapshot-with-provenance for cross-context copies

## Evidence
- WalletRepository.logTransaction append-only
- driver_approval_logs (0 orphans, P6-06)
- verifier R2 (no SQL outside Infra)
- docker-compose.prod.yml postgres:17 provisioned

## Related source code
- `src/repositories/WalletRepository.js`
- `src/repositories/DriverRepository.js`
- `src/infrastructure/repositories/userReadModelAdapter.js`

## Related tests
- `tests/unit/repositories.test.js`

## Compliance percentage
**30%** — honest estimate: weight of ADR requirements realized under the ADR-005
layered architecture with evidence, vs. total requirements named in the ADR.

## Risks
- SQLite single-writer underpins C-1 defect class (ADR-001)
- No formal retention/erasure lifecycle

## Required actions
- Approve ADR-001 + Postgres cutover (G3)
- Tag data classes as contexts migrate
